message:
fix(packs/kubernetes): close safe-pattern bypass on get/describe/logs/etc in resource names

kubectl/helm safe patterns matched `\bget\b`, `\bdescribe\b`, `\blogs\b`,
etc. anywhere in the command, including inside hyphenated resource names.
So destructive commands on resources whose names contained one of those
keywords bypassed the destructive rules:

  kubectl delete deployment get-handler      -> matched kubectl-get safe
  kubectl delete statefulset describe-worker -> matched kubectl-describe
  kubectl delete daemonset logs-archive      -> matched kubectl-logs
  kubectl delete pvc top-disk                -> matched kubectl-top
  helm uninstall get-operator                -> matched helm-get

Changed trailing `\b` to `(?=\s|$)` across every single-token kubectl
and helm safe subcommand. Positional subcommands still short-circuit
(`kubectl get pods`, `helm list`), but hyphen-internal matches no
longer short-circuit destructive ops.

Regression test added in kubectl for four hyphenated-resource-name
shapes.
